titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale School of Medicine, Department of Pathology teaching collection of medical illustrations
Department of Pathology, Yale School of Medicine, teaching collection of medical illustrations
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 21, 2026 at 22:00

did.unittitle: Yale School of Medicine, Department of Pathology teaching collection of medical illustrations

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research. The bulk of materials have been digitized.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale School of Medicine Department of Pathology, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized by box, in the order in which it was received from the Yale School of Medicine Department of Pathology. Box 30 includes reference materials and records related to the collection.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale School of Medicine Department of Pathology teaching collection of medical illustrations. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials may be found in Ms Coll 26 the Harry Zimmerman collection of medical illustrations.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes medical illustrations created by Armin B. Hemberger (1896-19874), Elmira (Warner) Hemberger, and others for the Yale School of Medicine Department of Pathology. The illustrations were used by the Department of Pathology for medical instruction. Illustrations in the collection were rendered in pen and ink, pencil, gouache, and watercolor and have been mounted on board. Many items include annotations on the back that may include the artist's name, date of creation, or identification numbers.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

Higher magnification of one of the bronchi seen in Fig. 10. Note the early inflammatory reaction in one portion of the wall, and the degeneration of the lining cell, which have lost all the characteristics of bronchial epithelium.

Edema of Larynx
Experimental - 'Pellagra' - Acute Pharyngitis
Experimental Xerophthalmia. Dilation of Acini of Lacrimal Gland.

Extensive broncho - pneumonia in a dog dying four days after exposure. The bronchus in the center has lost its mucosa and the outer coats are being broken down. The alveolar septa are outlined by thick strings of fibrin which is deposited in them.

Fibrinous Pneumonia' in dog dying three days after gassing. This picture is the exception, the pneumonia showing generally alveoli full of cells with the fibrin deposited chiefly in and on the alveolar walls.
Infarct of the Kidney
Influenza. Broncho-Pneumonia Hemorrhagic Exudate

a. Atrophic Skin Area with Mononuclear Infiltration of Dermis.

Cross Section of Uterine Wall Showing Carcinoma
Dilation of small bronchus with epithelial necrosis; small masses of bacteria (to left) with leucocytic infiltration. Death 72 hours after exposure.

Echinococcus Alveolaris in the Prostate.
Echinococcus Alveolaris Infection - Nephritis.
Edema and acute emphysema in acute stage.
Experimental Xerophthalmia.

Hemorrhages in Kidney of New Born
Infectious granuloma of unknown etiology. Mononuclear infiltration about and within the adrenal cortex.
Infectious granuloma of unknown etiology. Mononuclear infiltration of the kidney.
Infectious granuloma of unknown etiology. Mononuclear infiltration of the Pancreas.
Infectious granuloma of unknown etiology. Mononuclear infiltration of the thyroid.

Round Cell Infiltration of Dermis. Dilation of Blood Vessels. Hodgkin's Disease. Advanced Lesion with Overwhelming Number of Large Cells. Slight Fibrosis. Lymph Gland.
Sporotrichosis - skin nodule. Cell types.
Sporotrichosis. Skin nodule showing giant cell and small blood vessels within the granuloma.
Syphilitic Mesaortitis. Mural Thrombosis.
Trachea of same case, opened from behind. Frothy fluid pours from out of both bronchi. Tracheal mucosa is normal save for slight congestion of vessels in the intercartilaginous zones.
Types of atypical mitoses from a carcinoma of the oesophagus.
Uterus (Carcinoma)-Epithelium in Transitional Area
Water color for drawing of trachea of dog dying 25 hours after exposure; the epithelium is entirely destroyed, the submucosa is edematous with large fibrin spaces and many leucocytes

Lung of Dog Dying in Acute Stage After Gassing with Chlorine Shows Extreme Congestion. Moderate Edema and Alternating Patches of Acute Emphysema and Partial Atelectasis. Congestion and Edema are Most Pronounced in Collapsed Areas
Lung. A Small Bronchus is Completely Occluded by Sloughed Epithelium. The Outer Coat of a Small Artery is Extremely Edematous

Syphilitic Mesaortitis with Aneurysm Involving the Entire Aortic Arch. Viewed from the Left Side. Erosion of the Vertebral Bodies is Indicated in Two Areas in the Descending Aorta. The Point of Rupture of the Aneurysm into the Lung is Shown in the Foreground. Adhesion to the Sternum Anteriorly

Trachea and lungs of dog dying 12 hours after gassing. There is extreme edema, congestion and cyanosis of the lungs with patches of acute emphysema. Tracheal mucosa is nephrotic and covered by a sticky exudate.
